place: Wrightstown borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Wrightstown was named for early settler John Wright, who donated the land that became the borough.
The borough was incorporated on March 4, 1918, after a referendum on March 26, 1918 split portions of New Hanover and North Hanover Townships.
Wrightstown sits within the New Jersey Pinelands National Reserve, a 1.1‑million‑acre protected ecosystem and the nation’s first biosphere reserve.
Its borders touch New Hanover, North Hanover, Pemberton, Springfield, and the military bases of McGuire AFB and Fort Dix.
The borough follows New Jersey’s most common municipal government form, a council‑manager system used in 218 of the state’s 564 towns.
Wrightstown’s council consists of six members elected to staggered three‑year terms, with two seats up for election each year.
